name: Instagram
description: Instagram is a social media platform for sharing photos, videos, and stories. Only supports Instagram Business and Creator accounts, not Instagram Personal accounts.

tools.description: Create a draft carousel post with multiple images/videos before publishing.

child_image_urls.title: Child Image Urls
child_image_urls.description: List of image URLs to include as children

child_video_urls.title: Child Video Urls
child_video_urls.description: List of video URLs to include as children

name.title: Name
name.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service

s3key.title: S3Key
s3key.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.

mimetype.title: Mimetype
mimetype.description: The MIME type of the file

child_image_files.title: Child Image Files
child_image_files.description: List of local image files to include as children

name.title: Name
name.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service

s3key.title: S3Key
s3key.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.

mimetype.title: Mimetype
mimetype.description: The MIME type of the file

child_video_files.title: Child Video Files
child_video_files.description: List of local video files to include as children

tools.description: Create a draft media container for photos/videos/reels before publishing.

cover_url.title: Cover Url
cover_url.description: Optional cover image URL for reels/videos

image_url.title: Image Url
image_url.description: Public URL of the image. Must be a directly downloadable URL (no redirects) accessible to Meta crawlers. Supported formats: JPG, PNG (WebP not supported). Must pass correct Content-Type header, Content-Length header, max 8MB file size, minimum 320px width, aspect ratio between 4:5 and 1.91:1. URL must return the image file directly, not an HTML page.

video_url.title: Video Url
video_url.description: Public URL of the video. Must be a directly downloadable URL (no redirects) accessible to Meta crawlers. Supported formats: MP4, MOV. Must pass correct Content-Type header, Content-Length header, max 100MB file size for feed videos, max 1GB for IGTV, minimum 3 seconds duration. URL must return the video file directly, not an HTML page.

image_file.title: FileUploadable
image_file.description: Local image file to upload. File will be uploaded to a public URL automatically. Use this instead of image_url for local files.

media_type.title: Media Type
media_type.description: Explicit media type override, e.g., REELS or CAROUSEL.

video_file.title: FileUploadable
video_file.description: Local video file to upload. File will be uploaded to a public URL automatically. Use this instead of video_url for local files.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: What you want to post: 'photo', 'video', 'reel', or 'carousel_item' (for carousel drafts)

tools.description: Publish a draft media container to Instagram (final publishing step).

creation_id.title: Creation Id
creation_id.description: Draft ID returned from create actions

tools.description: Get details about a specific Instagram DM conversation (participants, etc).
Get comments on an Instagram post.

after.title: After
after.description: Cursor for pagination - get comments after this cursor

tools.description: Get Instagram post insights/analytics (impressions, reach, engagement, etc.).

metric.title: Metric
metric.description: Metrics to retrieve for the media. If not provided and metric_preset is not set, uses auto_safe preset. Allowed metrics vary by media_product_type: IMAGE/CAROUSEL: impressions, reach, likes, comments, saved, shares, engagement. VIDEO: reach, plays, likes, comments, saved, shares (impressions NOT supported). REELS: reach, plays, likes, comments, saved, shares, total_interactions, ig_reels_video_view_total_time, ig_reels_avg_watch_time. Stories: impressions, reach, replies, taps_forward, taps_back, exits. Common unsupported metrics: engagement on REELS (use total_interactions), impressions on VIDEO/Reels. If a metric is unsupported for the post type, API returns 400 error.

metric_preset.title: MetricPreset
metric_preset.description: Predefined metric sets for different media types to avoid API errors.

tools.description: Check the processing status of a draft post container.
Get Instagram user info including profile details and statistics.

ig_user_id.title: Ig User Id
ig_user_id.description: Instagram Business Account ID (optional, defaults to current user)

tools.description: Get Instagram account-level insights and analytics (profile views, reach, follower count, etc.). metric_type (time_series or total_value): When set to total_value, the API returns a total_value object instead of values. breakdown: Only applicable when metric_type=total_value and only for supported metrics. timeframe: Required for demographics-related metrics and overrides since/until for those metrics.

since.title: Since
since.description: Start of time range (inclusive) as a Unix timestamp (seconds). Accepts date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) which will be converted to timestamps.

until.title: Until
until.description: End of time range (inclusive) as a Unix timestamp (seconds). Accepts date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) which will be converted to timestamps.

items.title: UserInsightMetric
items.description: Valid metrics for Instagram account-level insights.

metric.title: Metric
metric.description: Metrics to retrieve for the user account. Allowed values: reach, follower_count, profile_views, website_clicks, accounts_engaged, total_interactions, get_directions_clicks, text_message_clicks, engaged_audience_demographics, reached_audience_demographics, follower_demographics, online_followers. Demographics metrics require timeframe parameter. If multiple metrics are provided, all must support the same period.

period.title: Period
period.description: Aggregation period for insights. Allowed values: day, week, days_28, lifetime. Allowed values depend on metric.

breakdown.title: Breakdown
breakdown.description: Breakdown to use when metric_type=total_value. Allowed values: contact_button_type, follow_type, media_product_type.

timeframe.title: Timeframe
timeframe.description: Timeframe for demographics-related metrics; overrides since/until for those metrics. Common values: last_14_days, last_30_days, last_90_days, prev_month, this_month, this_week.

ig_user_id.title: Ig User Id
ig_user_id.description: Instagram Business Account ID (optional, defaults to current user)

metric_type.title: Metric Type
metric_type.description: Aggregation type for results. Allowed values: time_series, total_value.

tools.description: Get Instagram user's media (posts, photos, videos).

after.title: After
after.description: Cursor for pagination - get media after this cursor

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Number of media items to return (max 100)

ig_user_id.title: Ig User Id
ig_user_id.description: Instagram Business Account ID (optional, defaults to current user)

tools.description: List all Instagram DM conversations for the authenticated user.
List all messages from a specific Instagram DM conversation.
Mark Instagram DM messages as read/seen for a specific user.
Reply to a comment on Instagram media.

ig_comment_id.title: Ig Comment Id
ig_comment_id.description: Instagram Comment ID to reply to

tools.description: Send an image via Instagram DM to a specific user.
Send a text message to an Instagram user via DM.

ig_user_id.title: Ig User Id
ig_user_id.description: Instagram Business Account ID (optional when using /me/messages)

reply_to_message_id.title: Reply To Message Id
reply_to_message_id.description: Optional message ID to reply to

name: Math-MCP
description: Enable your LLMs to perform accurate numerical calculations with a simple API. Leverage basic arithmetic and statistical functions to enhance your applications. Simplify complex mathematical tasks effortlessly and improve your model's capabilities.

tools.description: Adds two numbers together
Subtracts the second number from the first number
Multiplies two numbers together
Divides the first number by the second number
Adds any number of numbers together
Divides two numbers and returns the remainder
Calculates the arithmetic mean of a list of numbers

numbers.description: Array of numbers to find the mean of

tools.description: Calculates the median of a list of numbers

numbers.description: Array of numbers to find the median of

tools.description: Finds the most common number in a list of numbers

numbers.description: Array of numbers to find the mode of

tools.description: Finds the minimum value from a list of numbers

numbers.description: Array of numbers to find the minimum of

tools.description: Finds the maximum value from a list of numbers

numbers.description: Array of numbers to find the maximum of

tools.description: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer
Rounds a number up to the nearest integer
Rounds a number to the nearest integer
Calculates the sine of a number in radians

number.description: The number in radians to find the sine of

tools.description: Calculates the arcsine of a number in radians

number.description: The number to find the arcsine of

tools.description: Calculates the cosine of a number in radians

number.description: The number in radians to find the cosine of

tools.description: Calculates the arccosine of a number in radians

number.description: The number to find the arccosine of

tools.description: Calculates the tangent of a number in radians

number.description: The number in radians to find the tangent of

tools.description: Calculates the arctangent of a number in radians

number.description: The number to find the arctangent of

tools.description: Converts a radian value to its equivalent in degrees

number.description: The number in radians to convert to degrees

tools.description: Converts a degree value to its equivalent in radians

number.description: The number in degrees to convert to radians

name: Clay MCP
description: Access your network seamlessly with a simple and efficient server. Leverage a variety of tools to enhance your applications and workflows. Start integrating with your existing systems effortlessly.

tools.description: Get information about the current user

Search for contacts and return matching people. Use for questions about specific contacts or "who" questions (e.g. "Who did I meet most?" or "who works as an engineer?"). Returns actual contact records for queries needing specific people. Note: interaction counts represent total accumulated interactions and cannot be filtered by date ranges.

gte.description: The minimum age to filter for.

lte.description: The maximum age to filter for.

age.description: Filter by age if the user is asking about birthday or age.

name.description: A list of names for this contact.

sort.description: Sort the results by a field.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return. If the user is only asking about a count, you should request 0.

keywords.description: A list of keywords to search for, only use this field as a fallback if no other fields are provided.

distance.description: The distance radius to filter for in kilometers.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location the user is asking about.

longitude.description: The longitude of the location the user is asking about.

location.description: Filter by location if the user is asking about a location.

group_ids.description: The group ID or 'starred' to filter contacts by group.

note_date.description: Filter by the date a note was created.

email_count.description: Filter by the number of email interactions

event_count.description: Filter by the number of calendar event interactions

note_content.description: Filter by the content of a note.

active.description: Are they currently working at this company?

company.description: A list of possible company names.

position.description: A list of possible job position titles

last_email_date.description: Filter by last email interactions by date

last_event_date.description: Filter by last calendar event interactions by date

first_email_date.description: Filter by first email interactions by date

first_event_date.description: Filter by first calendar event interactions by date

active.description: Are they currently studying at this school?

degree.description: A list of possible degree titles

school.description: A list of possible school names.

gte.description: The earliest date to filter for previous birthdays.

lte.description: The latest date to filter for previous birthdays.

gte.description: The earliest date to filter for upcoming birthdays.

lte.description: The latest date to filter for upcoming birthdays.

text_message_count.description: Filter by the number of text message interactions

exclude_contact_ids.description: Contact IDs to exclude from the search results. Use this field when the user asks for 'more contacts', 'additional contacts', 'other contacts', or similar requests to get different results from a previous search.

last_interaction_date.description: Filter by last generic interactions by date

first_interaction_date.description: Filter by first generic interactions by date

last_text_message_date.description: Filter by last text message interactions by date

first_text_message_date.description: Filter by first text message interactions by date

tools.description: Get full details for a contact by id, including emails, social links, phone numbers, and notes.

contact_id.description: The ID of the contact to get details for.

tools.description: Create a new contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to create a completely new contact, not for adding contacts to groups.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

title.description: The job title of the contact.

website.description: The website addresses of the contact. These can be any valid web address. They must not be locations.

birthday.description: The birthday of the contact. Use the format YYYY-MM-DD, if no year is specified use 0, if no month or day is specified do not include.

linkedin.description: The LinkedIn handle of the contact.

last_name.description: The last name of the contact.

postal.description: The postal code of the contact.

region.description: The state or region of the contact.

street.description: The street address of the contact.

locations.description: The locations associated with the contact.

first_name.description: The first name of the contact.

tools.description: Update a contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to update a contact, not for adding contacts to groups.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

title.description: The job title of the contact.

website.description: The website addresses of the contact. These can be any valid web address. They must not be locations.

birthday.description: The birthday of the contact. Use the format YYYY-MM-DD, if no year is specified use 0, if no month or day is specified do not include.

linkedin.description: The LinkedIn handle of the contact.

last_name.description: The last name of the contact.

postal.description: The postal code of the contact.

region.description: The state or region of the contact.

street.description: The street address of the contact.

locations.description: The locations associated with the contact.

contact_id.description: The ID of the contact to update.

first_name.description: The first name of the contact.

tools.description: Archive a contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to archive a contact.

contact_ids.description: The IDs of the contacts to perform the action on.

tools.description: Restore a archived contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to restore a contact.

contact_ids.description: The IDs of the contacts to perform the action on.

tools.description: Create a note for a contact. Only use this when the user explicitly asks to create, add, or save a note.

content.description: The content of the note. You may reference and link to other contacts in the note using the following format: [contact:{contact_id}:contact name], example: [contact:123:John Doe]

contact_id.description: The ID of the contact to add the note to.

reminder_date.description: If the user wants to set a reminder for the note. This should be a a ISO 8601 date in the following format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM[:SS[.sss]]Z

tools.description: Get all groups or lists for the user.

limit.description: The maximum number of groups to return.

tools.description: Create a group or list for the user. If a group with the same name already exists, it will not create a duplicate unless explicitly requested to ignore the check.

title.description: The name of the group to create.

tools.description: Update a group or list. Use this to update the group title and/or modify its members. When adding or removing contacts, provide ALL contact IDs to add/remove in a single call - do not make multiple calls for individual contacts.

title.description: The new name of the group if the user wants to rename it.

group_id.description: The ID of the group or list to update.

add_contact_ids.description: A list of contact IDs that should be added to this group. Each ID should be a number representing an existing contact in your network. You can provide multiple IDs to add several contacts at once.

remove_contact_ids.description: A list of contact IDs that should be removed from this group. Each ID should be a number representing an existing contact in your network. You can provide multiple IDs to remove several contacts at once.

tools.description: Use ONLY when the user explicitly mentions "note" or "notes" to retrieve notes between two dates (e.g. "what notes from last week?"). Returns notes by creation date only - does NOT search note content or filter by other criteria. NEVER use this tool for finding contacts or any other purpose besides retrieving notes. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "note" or "notes" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch meetings/events in a date range (e.g. "what meetings next week?", "show calendar for tomorrow"). DO NOT use for counting meetings, analyzing patterns, or finding frequent participants.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch upcoming/next meetings/events when the user does not explicitly mention a range, and asks for upcoming or next meetings/events.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch emails in a date range (e.g. "what emails did I recieve last week?", "show emails from yesterday")

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch recent email interactions when the user does not explicitly mention a range, and asks for past email interactions.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool to find recent reminders that have already occured. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "reminder" or "reminders" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Use this tool to find recent reminders that have already occured. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "reminder" or "reminders" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

contact_ids.description: The contacts to filter the moments for.

tools.description: Find potential duplicate contacts.
Merge all contact ids into one contact, this is a destructive action and can not be undone. Only run one contact group at a time and only with the users explicit permission.
